instructions.name: Maestro Venture Onboarding
instructions.objective: Deep-dive with the CEO to understand a new company, product, or effort — then create the foundational briefing that every agent will operate from

llm.mandate: This workflow is INTERACTIVE — Maestro asks, CEO answers, Maestro probes deeper
Do NOT rush — the quality of this onboarding determines the quality of ALL downstream work
Every question should build on the CEO's previous answers
Security and compliance implications must be surfaced early

step.goal: Understand what we're building and why
step.action: Display: "⚔️ VENTURE ONBOARDING — Let's understand your vision, {user_name}."
Display: "I'm going to ask you a series of questions. Answer at whatever level of detail you have — I'll dig deeper where it matters."
step.ask: What are we building? Give me the elevator pitch — what is this company/product/effort?
What problem does this solve? Who feels this pain most acutely?
Why now? What's changed that makes this the right time?
What's the business model? How does this make money (or save money, or create value)?
Who are the competitors? What exists today that people use instead?
What's your unfair advantage? Why will YOU win this?

step.goal: Understand the target users and their world
step.action: Display: "⚔️ Now let's understand your users."
step.ask: Who is the primary user? Describe them — their role, their day, their frustrations.
Are there secondary users or stakeholders who aren't the primary user but matter?
What does success look like for the user? When they use this, what changes for them?
What's the user's technical sophistication? Are they developers, business users, consumers?
Any accessibility requirements or constraints for the user base?

step.goal: Understand the product scope and priorities
step.ask: What are the MUST-HAVE features for launch? The absolute minimum viable product.
What are the NICE-TO-HAVE features? Things you want but can live without initially.
What's explicitly OUT OF SCOPE? Things you do NOT want to build (at least not now).
Any existing code, designs, or prototypes we're building on? Or is this greenfield?
What platforms? Web, mobile, desktop, API, CLI — where does this live?
Any specific technology preferences or constraints? Languages, frameworks, cloud providers?

step.goal: Understand security and compliance requirements early
step.action: Display: "⚔️ Security and compliance — we handle this upfront, not as an afterthought."
step.ask: What kind of data will this handle? Personal data, financial data, health data, proprietary data?
Any regulatory requirements? HIPAA, SOC 2, GDPR, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, NIST, industry-specific?
Who are the threat actors? Who would want to attack this and why?
Multi-tenant or single-tenant? How is data isolated between customers?
Any specific security requirements from customers, partners, or insurers?
step.action: Based on answers, flag which compliance agents will be needed: - Health data → Deploy Dr. Vita (Healthcare Expert) and Shield (Security Auditor) - Government data → Deploy Senator (Government Expert) and Atlas (NIST RMF) - Financial data → Deploy Sterling (Financial Expert) and Shield - PII/GDPR → Deploy Shield at minimum - No special requirements → Deploy Shield for baseline security review

step.goal: Understand how the CEO wants to work
step.action: Display: "⚔️ How do you want to run this operation?"
step.ask: What's your timeline? Any hard deadlines or milestones?
How involved do you want to be in day-to-day decisions vs. strategic direction only?
Any team members (human or otherwise) already involved that I should know about?
What's your risk tolerance? Move fast and break things, or careful and methodical?
What does v1 success look like? When do we celebrate?

step.goal: Synthesize everything into the venture brief and cascade to agents
step.action: Display: "⚔️ Synthesizing your vision into the venture brief..."
step.template-output: Generate the complete venture brief from all CEO answers. This is THE foundational document — every agent's work traces back to this.

step.goal: Create initial mission briefings for relevant agents based on the venture
step.action: Display: "⚔️ Briefing the team on the new mission..."

Based on the venture brief, determine which agents are immediately relevant.
Create mission briefings (team/_memory/{agent-name}/mission.md) for each:
ALWAYS DEPLOY: - Winston (Architect) → Initial architecture decisions based on venture requirements - John (PM) → PRD creation from the venture brief - Sally (UX Designer) → Initial UX research and user flow design - Amelia (Dev) → Technology stack setup and project scaffolding - Murat (Tea) → Test strategy and framework setup - Shield (Security Auditor) → Security architecture review - Paige (Tech Writer) → Documentation structure and standards
DEPLOY IF APPLICABLE (based on Phase 4 answers): - Atlas (NIST RMF) → If NIST compliance needed - Dr. Vita (Healthcare) → If health data involved - Senator (Government) → If government sector - Sterling (Financial) → If financial data involved - Forge (DevOps) → If infrastructure/deployment is complex - Pact (API Contract) → If API-first or microservices architecture - Nexus (Agentic Expert) → If AI/agentic features planned - Neuron (ML Expert) → If ML/model training involved - Oracle (Data Architect) → If complex data modeling needed
Each mission briefing must reference the venture brief and include SPECIFIC first tasks.

Write Maestro's own briefing to team/_memory/maestro/venture-context.md with: - Venture name and summary - Key decisions made - Agents deployed - CEO's priorities and risk tolerance - Timeline and milestones - What to surface to CEO vs. handle autonomously

step.goal: Present the recommended execution plan to the CEO
step.action:
Display: "⚔️ VENTURE ONBOARDED | {venture_name} ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Venture brief saved to: {planning_artifacts}/venture-brief-{venture_name}-{date}.md Agents briefed: [list of agents with mission briefings]
RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS (in order): 1. [First recommendation — typically PRD creation with John (PM)] 2. [Second — typically Architecture with Winston] 3. [Third — typically UX research with Sally] 4. [Fourth — typically Sprint Planning with Maestro] 5. [Fifth — typically first story creation]
Say [LR] to run a full scan now that the venture is onboarded. Or pick an agent to start with.
Your company, your call, {user_name}. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════"
